user_prompt: start the serial port, and then just test pbkdf2 with ever growing number of rounds, timing each iteration using millisecond precision
increase by 20% each time, so next one is like 12
once it reaches 256, start servicing the watchdog
once it reaches 2 seconds for the entire process, restart the test, but this time, do not service the watchdog
agent_reply: - Prints per-case elapsed time in milliseconds, cumulative time, success flag, and first digest byte
- Uses the watchdog-servicing production PBKDF2 path only once rounds are `>= 256`
- Stops the first phase once cumulative benchmark time reaches `>= 2000 ms`
- Restarts the benchmark with raw mbedTLS PBKDF2 and no watchdog servicing, where a WDT reset is expected
Build passed. Only warning was an unrelated deprecated `NetworkServer::available()` warning from `ESP-FTP-Server`.

commit_message: file download and decrypted downloads work now

user_prompt: point me at all the places where an intentional reset is about to happen
agent_reply: I found these whole-device intentional reset paths:
`requestRebootWithFlag(uint32_t flag)` sets `reset_magic` / `reset_flag`, waits 10 ms, then calls `ESP.restart()`. I did not find any current callers.
`reboot_after_reset_dialog()` logs “reset confirmation dismissed; rebooting”, delays, then `esp_restart()`. Used after password reset and memory reset dialogs.
When firmware update view is in `Complete` state, touch press/release delays then `esp_restart()`.
If cloud upload completes but modal/gui is unavailable, it logs “rebooting”, delays, then `esp_restart()`.
Exiting cloud scroll context reboots by policy after Wi-Fi/cloud use.
Exiting Wi-Fi scroll after Wi-Fi has started reboots by policy.
Cloud upload confirmation dismissed: logs, delays, `esp_restart()`.
Pairing confirmation dismissed: logs “rebooting to restart Bluetooth cleanly”, flushes serial, delays, `esp_restart()`.
One test intentionally expects a watchdog reset rather than directly calling restart:
Prints “WDT reset is expected” before running PBKDF without watchdog servicing.
I ignored ordinary “reset internal state” functions, FIFO resets, decoder resets, and `esp_task_wdt_reset()` because those are not whole-device resets.
